name: Yui Tanaka
description: Mid-30s. Half-Japanese, half-Filipino, born in Daly City, lives in Millbrae. Hair in a tight bun. Tired around the eyes. Has worked the SFO–TPE rotation for six years and knows the upper-deck galley like her own kitchen.
voice: Efficient, professional, slight clipped quality when tired.
signature: Recognizes regular passengers without seeming to, by gait and posture more than face.
Has a single visible tell when stressed — adjusts her cuff with her thumb.
Will go out of her way for a passenger she perceives as genuinely ill or grieving.
dmNotes:
Holds PC1's bag at the SFO gate in Episode 1's Scene 7 retrocausal play-out — the thing that should never happen.  In Episode 2's gate-agent investigation she is the second NPC the PCs interview; she has no memory of holding the bag.  Her memory is genuinely missing; she did not lie.  She is the parallel-denial confirmation that one PC's gate-edit recollection is not just an isolated bad memory.

If the PCs treat her with respect during the Episode 2 interview, she may become an ongoing texture NPC — Yui or her wife Inez surface in later episodes as a reminder of the cost paid by NPCs who never know they paid it.  She does not become a caster or a network member; she remains a civilian whose life was edited around her.

background.household: Wife Inez (graphic designer). Three-year-old daughter Mei. Lives near SFO for the commute.
background.hobbies: Bakes sourdough on her days off. Trying to learn the koto, badly.
background.career: Started with the airline at 27 after a brief stint in restaurant management. Liked the discipline of cabin crew work. Has been quietly applying for purser positions for two years.
background.currentStress: Mei was up most of last night with a cold. Yui has been awake since 0400. Her mother is watching Mei tonight; Yui will call from Taipei tomorrow morning.
